methods.description: Process for determining candidate epitopes by analyzing signal intensity values and identifying peptides with significant signal deviations.

steps.description: Candidate epitopes were defined by being represented by at least three adjacent peptides with a signal at least two standard deviations above the background signal for that sample.
steps.searchable_summary: identify candidate epitopes based on signal deviation

methods.description: Alignment of amino acid sequences of homologous grass pollen allergens to allow for comparison across different species and variants.

steps.description: The amino acid sequences of all allergens belonging to the same group were aligned using MacVector Inc. (version 18.0.0).
steps.searchable_summary: align amino acid sequences using MacVector

steps.description: ClustalW was applied, using the Gonnet substitution matrix, open and extended gap penalty set to 10.00 and 0.20, respectively, and the slow pairwise alignment mode.
steps.searchable_summary: apply ClustalW with specific parameters for sequence alignment

steps.description: Each peptide was assigned a number representing the position of its first amino acid, according to the alignment, and visualizations were made based on these numbers.
steps.searchable_summary: assign position numbers to peptides and create visualizations

methods.description: Alignment and renumbering of control allergens not included in the AIT, using sequences from UniProt.

steps.description: Sequences of the human 60S acidic ribosomal protein 1 (P05386) and the human 60S acidic ribosomal protein 2 (P05387) were obtained from UniProt.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch human ribosomal protein sequences from UniProt

steps.description: Acidic ribosomal protein 1 allergens and acidic ribosomal protein 2 allergens were aligned and renumbered accordingly.
